_meta.unit: A2.5 WO5K-battles (PASS 3 — the Spicer betrayal mechanism + the Fords/Duskendale army-thinning)
_meta.note: 26th major-arc enrichment dip; PASS 3 (the UNRAVELLING) of the multi-pass A2.5 mini-track (the LAST A-roundup unit). TWO halves. HALF A = THE SPICER BETRAYAL MECHANISM revealed in AFFC (affc-jaime-07): Tywin engineered Robb's fatal marriage from within via Lady Sybell Spicer -- minted the betrayal substrate (tywin MANIPULATES sybell via_bribe [the pardon+Castamere+marriages package]; sybell BETRAYS robb) + a NEW event hub jeyne-westerling-kept-barren [event.incident, [wo5k]] reifying the covert sabotage (sybell AGENT_IN / jeyne VICTIM_IN / tywin COMMANDS_IN) + grey-wind FORESHADOWS it (un-parks harvest row 999, asos-catelyn-02:189). The moon-tea MECHANISM stays GATED/node-prose-free (book says only 'I made certain of that, as your lord father bid me'). DEDUP: sybell CONSPIRES_WITH tywin + rolph HOLDS_TITLE lord-of-castamere + castamere SEAT_OF house-spicer + grey-wind OPPOSES rolph-spicer + the whole marriage spine all EXIST -- NOT re-minted. HALF B = THE ARMY-THINNING LOSSES that gut Robb's host before the RW -- lit the two causally-ISLANDED, roster-EMPTY battle hubs: battle-at-duskendale (Randyll Tarly shatters Glover & Tallhart -- a third of Robb's foot; canonical slug confirmed vs the historical defiance-/sack-of-duskendale) and fighting-at-the-fords-of-the-trident (the ASOS Roose/Gregor Ruby-Ford fight; canonical slug confirmed -- NOT battle-of-the-fords, the distinct ACOK Edmure/Tywin Red-Fork battle, and NOT the same_as redirect slugs). Gave each its first roster (Tarly/Gregor COMMANDS_IN, Glover/Tallhart/Wylis-Manderly VICTIM_IN, Roose COMMANDS_IN the ford crossing, LOCATED_AT) + the WHODUNIT (roose SUSPECTED_OF both -- Duskendale strong [Robb's bafflement + all-non-Bolton dead], Ruby-Ford weaker/structural [flagged for fresh-verify]; tier-2 cap). CAUSAL WIRING (Lens 4): both hubs ENABLES red-wedding-conspiracy (the thinned host made the betrayal decisive -- mirrors the existing karstark-host-deserts-robb ENABLES red-wedding-conspiracy precedent). DROPPED roose COMMANDS_IN battle-at-duskendale (the 'Why would they go?' quote supports the SUSPICION not proven command -- folded into SUSPECTED_OF) + gregor AGENT_IN duskendale + harrion VICTIM_IN duskendale (both wiki-only, no book quote). The 7 verify:true edges (MANIPULATES/BETRAYS interpretive labels + the FORESHADOWS + the 2 SUSPECTED_OF + the 2 ENABLES) carry pending for the independent fresh-verify. Container [wo5k] on the 2 battle hubs handled by node-edit (they pre-exist); the new kept-barren node ships [wo5k]. EXCLUDES TWOW; theory-gated clean (moon-tea NOT asserted). PASS 3 closes the WO5K-battles mini-track; A2.8 Davos/Sam residual closes the A-roundup. Also un-parks harvest row 983 (Frey-pact foreshadow -> quote-attach onto robb-weds-jeyne-westerling, handled by node-edit).

edges.quote: I made certain of that, as your lord father bid me.
edges.note: Tywin used Lady Sybell as his instrument inside the Westerling marriage, bought with a reward package -- a royal pardon for House Westerling, Castamere for her brother Rolph, and worthy marriages for her daughters (affc-jaime-07:81-83). 'as your lord father bid me' = she acted on Tywin's direction. Distinct from the EXISTING symmetric sybell-spicer CONSPIRES_WITH tywin-lannister: MANIPULATES names Tywin as the directing/senior party who set the terms; qualifier via_bribe (the closest enum fit -- the rewards were the lever; via_promise is not in the locked enum).

edges.quote: I made certain of that, as your lord father bid me.
edges.note: Sybell -- Robb's good-mother, living under his protection after the Crag fell -- sabotaged his marriage from within on Tywin's orders (ensured no Stark heir). BETRAYS names Robb as the victim; distinct from the EXISTING symmetric sybell CONSPIRES_WITH tywin (which captures the Sybell<->Tywin dyad, not the victim).

edges.quote: I made certain of that, as your lord father bid me.
edges.note: Sybell is the active agent of the keeping-barren act -- 'I made certain of that' is unambiguous first-person agency. Role edge on the new event hub.

edges.quote: You had no right. Robb had it made for me. I loved him.
edges.note: Jeyne is the unwitting patient of the kept-barren act (stated by Sybell at affc-jaime-07:79). This quote (her grief over the crown her mother tore from her, :73) establishes she was a true victim who loved Robb -- not a party to her mother's scheme. Role edge on the new event hub.

edges.quote: I made certain of that, as your lord father bid me.
edges.note: 'as your lord father bid me' -- Tywin ordered the covert sabotage; orderer role on the hub (he directed it, did not personally execute it). Corroborated by Jaime's gloss 'Tywin Lannister was not a man to overlook such details' (:80).

edges.quote: He bares his teeth every time Ser Rolph comes near him.
edges.note: Grey Wind's distrust of Ser Rolph Spicer (Sybell's brother, the proximate Spicer in Robb's circle) foreshadows the hidden Spicer betrayal embedded in the marriage. Reader-craft edge (Detail -> Event). Resolves parked harvest row asos-catelyn-02:189 (the unheeded wolf-warning).

edges.quote: where Lord Randyll Tarly had shattered Robett Glover and Ser Helman Tallhart
edges.note: Tarly commanded the Lannister force that shattered the northern host at Duskendale -- 'shattered' is the book's own outcome verb. First participant role on the causally-ISLANDED, roster-EMPTY hub.

edges.quote: where Lord Randyll Tarly had shattered Robett Glover and Ser Helman Tallhart
edges.note: Glover's host was shattered at Duskendale; he survived and was put on a ship as a captive (asos-catelyn-05:29). VICTIM_IN -- the losing/suffering commander.

edges.quote: where Lord Randyll Tarly had shattered Robett Glover and Ser Helman Tallhart
edges.note: Tallhart's host was shattered alongside Glover's at Duskendale (he was killed there). VICTIM_IN.
The hub had NO location edge (baseline gap). The battle is named for and occurred at Duskendale.

edges.quote: Gregor Clegane attacked with heavy horse and drove them into the river.
edges.note: Gregor led the Lannister heavy-horse assault on the fording rear-guard at the Ruby Ford. First participant role on the causally-ISLANDED, roster-EMPTY hub.

edges.quote: I had no choice but to ferry my men across in small boats, of which we had too few.
edges.note: Roose's own first-person account of commanding the northern host's contested crossing of the Trident at the Ruby Ford. COMMANDS_IN -- he commanded the army throughout, even caught on the wrong bank.

edges.quote: with Ser Wylis Manderly and his White Harbor knights as rear guard
edges.note: Wylis Manderly commanded the rear-guard caught crossing; driven into the river and taken captive (his brother Wendel: 'My brother is taken again', :245). VICTIM_IN.

edges.quote: crossed the Trident at the ruby ford
edges.note: The hub had NO location edge (baseline gap). The fighting occurred at the Ruby Ford crossing of the Trident.

edges.quote: Why would they go to Duskendale?
edges.note: Roose (Robb's Harrenhal commander) is suspected of having sent Glover & Tallhart to their destruction at Duskendale to thin the Stark-loyal infantry ahead of his defection. In-text basis: Robb's bafflement signals he never authorized the march ('Why would they go to Duskendale?' / 'A third of my foot, lost for Duskendale?'), and the dead were all non-Bolton bannermen while Roose's own Dreadfort men were spared. UNPROVEN in the 5 books (no confession) -- SUSPECTED_OF capped tier-2. STRONG suspicion.

edges.quote: I blame myself. I delayed too long before leaving Harrenhal.
edges.note: Roose is suspected of deliberately exposing the Stark-loyal rear-guard (Norrey/Locke/Burley/Manderly) at the Ruby Ford while his own Dreadfort men survived intact; his self-exculpatory 'I delayed too long' account reads as engineered. WEAKER / structural basis than the Duskendale suspicion (his account frames the timing as misfortune; no in-text 'why would they go?' moment) -- flagged for fresh-verify to gate. SUSPECTED_OF capped tier-2.

edges.quote: A third of my foot, lost for Duskendale?
edges.note: The Duskendale loss destroyed roughly a third of Robb's infantry, thinning his host and making the Red-Wedding betrayal militarily decisive. ENABLES, not CAUSES -- the attrition removed a deterrent; the conspiracy was a free Frey/Bolton choice. Mirrors the EXISTING karstark-host-deserts-robb ENABLES red-wedding-conspiracy precedent exactly.

edges.quote: Two-thirds of my strength was on the north side when the Lannisters attacked those still waiting to cross.
edges.note: The Ruby Ford loss of Stark-loyal rear-guard infantry -- and the concentration of Roose's own unthinned Dreadfort men in the surviving host that marched to the Twins -- further preconditioned the conspiracy. ENABLES, not CAUSES. A DISTINCT precondition from Duskendale (rear-guard vs centre); not double-counting.
